cards.back: A branch of digital electronics that deals with the flow of digital signals over time, enabling the storage and manipulation of information in digital circuits.
A digital circuit that processes and stores information in a sequential manner, using components such as counters, regulators, and memory elements.
A basic storage element in digital electronics that can store a digital value and maintain it even if the clock signal is absent or delayed.
A more advanced storage element that can store a digital value and exhibit a memory-like behavior, allowing it to retain its state even when the clock signal is absent.
Threshold-Triggered Logic (TTL) is a family of logic gates and other digital circuits that use a threshold voltage to trigger a logic level change.
